steps.instruction: Place a Clock element on the canvas
steps.hint: Click the I/O tab on the left panel, then drag the Clock element onto the canvas.

steps.instruction: Place an LED on the canvas
steps.hint: Still in the I/O tab, drag a Led element onto the canvas to the right of the Clock.

steps.instruction: Connect the Clock output to the LED input
steps.hint: Click and drag from the Clock's output port (right side) to the LED's input port (left side).

steps.instruction: The waveform viewer is now open — take a look!
steps.hint: BeWavedDolphin shows your circuit signals as rows. The Clock row is the input, and the Led row is the output. Each column is one simulation time step.
Click Edit → Combinational in BeWavedDolphin's menu bar. This fills the Clock row with all possible values and computes the LED output for each — a full truth table in one click.
Double-click any cell in the Clock row to flip it between 0 and 1. Watch the Led row update instantly to reflect the new output.
